findings.description: Header information (ROY COOPER/Governor, ELIZABETH S. BISER/Secretary, KATHY B. RAWLS/Director) is duplicated at the start of the HTML — once as part of the letterhead rendering and again as text content — with a page footer block inserted between them, disrupting reading order.
findings.evidence: PDF page 1 shows the letterhead header once at the top. HTML chunk 1 renders the header info twice: first near the logo image, then after a footer block ('State of North Carolina | Division of Marine Fisheries…'), the same names/titles appear again before the PT-2-2024 identifier and PROCLAMATION heading.

findings.description: A full-width image (611.8pt) is embedded between sections IX and X with empty alt text (alt=""). If this is a substantive image (e.g., a map), the empty alt text fails to describe it; if it is a page-break artifact, it should not be present at all.
findings.evidence: HTML: placed inside the Brunswick County (IX) list item after item IX.E. No corresponding visible image appears between sections IX and X in the provided 3-page PDF.

findings.description: A full-page-width image is placed mid-content inside the Brunswick County (section IX) list item, between the last sub-item (IX.E, Bonaparte Creek) and section X (General Information). In the PDF, only a page break and repeated page footer separate these sections. This appears to be a page-break/footer artifact embedded in the body flow, disrupting reading continuity.
findings.evidence:
HTML: is nested inside
  • for section IX, placed after IX.E and before section X. PDF pages 2-3 show only a standard page footer between these sections.
